title: E2E Coverage Ledger
description: One record per end-to-end completion run against a real deployed system. Sits on the helix arc (Loop -> Spiral -> Double-Helix -> Evolution) as the layer that proves an e2e was *completed*, not merely declared. The teeth (enforced by scripts/e2e_coverage_check.py, not by presence): the covered surfaces are discovered from code/deploy and every discovered surface must be in the ledger (closure); presence is separated from exercise so render-only cannot pass a mutation/auth/stateful surface; every such surface carries a boundary-companion battery; RED findings carry a concrete repro + tracker link; residue is zero; no-mock/real-backend is asserted with read-back evidence; and no raw secret/token may appear anywhere in the ledger.

run_id.description: Stable identifier for this e2e run.

target.description: System under test (domain-agnostic; e.g. an app/service name).

tier.description: Must be a real backend tier; a mock/stub tier is not a completion environment.

base_ref.description: Concrete reference to the environment exercised (deploy URL / revision).

asserted.description: Real backend, no mocks. A run that mocks the system under test is not a completion.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence that real store/state effects were observed. At least one entry must show an actual read-back (file:line, or a read-back/re-query/persisted/stored/record/round-trip keyword) -- a bare tracker URL does not prove a real backend.

discovered.description: Surfaces discovered from code/deploy (routes/panels/endpoints/actions) -- NOT just the provided test list.

source_ref.description: Concrete provenance: file:line / route path / deploy ref.

provided_list_ref.description: Concrete reference to the provided test inventory, if any.

missing_from_provided.description: Discovered surface ids absent from the provided list (the Negative-Space closure finding). Each entry MUST be an id present in surface_inventory.discovered (not prose or a ghost id) -- so a 'missing' surface is a real, then-covered surface. May be empty, but the field is mandatory so the audit is recorded.

coverage.description: One entry per discovered surface. Every discovered surface MUST appear here (closure).

verified.description: For mutation/auth/stateful surfaces this MUST be true: render/presence alone does not exercise the surface.

kind.description: What was actually exercised. For mutation/auth/stateful surfaces it must name a real exercise (create-readback-delete, round-trip, stateful-continuity, boundary-probe, mutate-verify, replay); a render/presence/screenshot-only kind is rejected even when verified is true.

boundary_companions.description: Required for mutation/auth/stateful surfaces. Each companion is either a concrete evidence ref (string) or { na: true, reason: <substantive why-not-applicable> }. The class's CORE companions can never be N/A (mutation: auth_reject/authz_rbac/idempotency; auth: auth_reject/authz_rbac; stateful: auth_reject/idempotency); other N/As need a non-vague reason. The battery cannot be opted out via prose.

red_findings.description: Real failures found. A RED cannot be closed without a concrete repro and a tracker link; the run cannot be reported complete with an unresolved RED lacking an issue.

issue_ref.description: Tracker URL (issue/PR/ticket link) -- must be a URL, not a file path or route. An untracked RED cannot be reported as handled.

count.description: Test data left behind. Must be zero; an e2e that pollutes the environment is not complete.

evidence_refs.description: Concrete artifacts (screenshots / API read-backs / logs) backing the run.

secret_handling.description: How credentials/sessions were obtained and protected (raw-byte handling, no-leak). MUST NOT contain a raw secret value.

safety_floor_preserved.description: Reachability/session-mint never relaxes the safety floor (auth/authz still enforced; no secret leaked).

reviews.description: Same #43 review contract as the spiral/evolution ledgers: >= 2 distinct registered reviewers; a no_block needs a concrete refute_pass; no self-review.
